revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iwaya-jinja (Akashi)]][[ja:岩屋神社 (明石市)]] {{Expand Japanese|岩屋神社 (明石市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iwaya Shrine | native_name = 岩屋神社 | image = Iwaya Shrine Akashi.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iwaya Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.643944|134.988194}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|lt=Izanagi|ar|إزاناغي|az|İzanaqi|bcl|Izanagi|bn|ইজানাগি|bs|Izanagi|ca|Izanagi|cs|Izanagi a Izanami|da|Izanagi|el|Ιζανάγκι|en|Izanagi|eo|Izanagi|es|Izanagi|fa|ایزاناگی|fi|Izanaki|fr|Izanagi|he|איזאנגי|hr|Izanagi|hu|Izanagi|id|Izanagi|it|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|ka|იძანაგი|ko|이자나기|lt|Idzanagis|mk|Изанаги|mni|ꯏꯓꯥꯅꯥꯒꯤ|no|Izanagi|or|ଇଜାନାଗି|pt|Izanagi|ro|Izanagi|ru|Идзанаги|simple|Izanagi|sk|Izanagi|sl|Izanagi|sr|Изанаки|sv|Izanagi|ta|இசநாகி|th|อิซานางิ|tl|Izanagi|tr|İzanagi|uk|Ідзанаґі|vi|Izanagi|zh|伊邪那岐|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iwaya Shrine'''|岩屋神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * Province}} ** Series ordinal: 7 ** P155: {{ill|Akaba Shrine|lt=Akaba Shrine|ja|赤羽神社|qid=Q20042898|1=Akaba Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11505275|ltq=Q11505275|qid=Q11505275|1=Hioka Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Akashi, Hyōgo|lt=Akashi|ar|أكاشي (هيوغو)|arz|اكاشى, هيوجو|azb|آکاشی، هیوقو|az|Akaşi|bg|Акаши|ca|Akashi|ceb|Akashi (lungsod sa Hapon)|ce|Акаси (гӀала)|de|Akashi|en|Akashi, Hyōgo|envoyage|Akashi|eo|Akaŝi|es|Akashi (Hyōgo)|et|Akashi|fa|آکاشی، هیوگو|fi|Akashi|fr|Akashi|frvoyage|Akashi|ga|Akashi|gl|Akashi|id|Akashi, Hyōgo|it|Akashi|ja|明石市|ko|아카시시|lt|Akašis|ms|Akashi, Hyōgo|mzn|آکاشی، هیوگو|nl|Akashi|no|Akashi|pl|Akashi|pt|Akashi|ptvoyage|Akashi|ro|Akashi|ru|Акаси (город)|sh|Akashi, Hjōgo|simple|Akashi, Hyōgo|sr|Акаши|sv|Akashi|tg|Акаши|th|อากาชิ|tl|Akashi, Hyogo|tr|Akashi|tt|Акаси (шәһәр)|uk|Акасі (Хіоґо)|ur|آکاشی, ہیوگو|uz|Akasi|vi|Akashi, Hyōgo|war|Akashi, Hyōgo|zh_classical|明石市|zh_min_nan|Akasi-chhī|zh_yue|明石|zh|明石市|qid=Q26406|1=Akashi, Hyōgo}} * {{ill|Q1047523|ltq=Q1047523|qid=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}} * {{ill|Q11512561|ltq=Q11512561|qid=Q11512561|1=Akashi District, Hyōgo}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.643944|134.988194}} == image (P18) == * Iwaya Shrine Akashi.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Izanagi|lt=Izanagi|ar|إزاناغي|az|İzanaqi|bcl|Izanagi|bn|ইজানাগি|bs|Izanagi|ca|Izanagi|cs|Izanagi a Izanami|da|Izanagi|el|Ιζανάγκι|en|Izanagi|eo|Izanagi|es|Izanagi|fa|ایزاناگی|fi|Izanaki|fr|Izanagi|he|איזאנגי|hr|Izanagi|hu|Izanagi|id|Izanagi|it|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|ka|იძანაგი|ko|이자나기|lt|Idzanagis|mk|Изанаги|mni|ꯏꯓꯥꯅꯥꯒꯤ|no|Izanagi|or|ଇଜାନାଗି|pt|Izanagi|ro|Izanagi|ru|Идзанаги|simple|Izanagi|sk|Izanagi|sl|Izanagi|sr|Изанаки|sv|Izanagi|ta|இசநாகி|th|อิซานางิ|tl|Izanagi|tr|İzanagi|uk|Ідзанаґі|vi|Izanagi|zh|伊邪那岐|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183013 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|ltq=Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いわやじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 673-0893 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3140005005589 == Commons category (P373) == * Iwaya-jinja (Akashi) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Iwatsuhimeno Shrine|lt=Iwatsuhimeno Shrine|qid=Q135041149|1=Iwatsuhimeno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県明石市材木町8-10', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '兵庫県明石市大蔵本町6-10', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Iwaya-jinja (Akashi)}} [[JA:岩屋神社 (明石市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11474394}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Iwaya Shrine |Image = [[File:Iwaya Shrine Akashi City.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Akashi City|Q26406|qid=Q26406|1=Akashi, Hyōgo}} 8-10 Zaimokuchō |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|38|38.2|N|134|59|17.5|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|qid=Q813858}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor), a disputed shrine
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (traditional) 13th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}} |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Iwaya Ebisu |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|qid=Q2925|1=October 13}} |Rituals = Oshataka Boat Ritual (3rd Sunday of July) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Iwaya Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[Image:Iwaya Jinja Komainu 2.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Komainu]] [[Image:Iwaya Jinja Komainu 1.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Komainu]] '''Iwaya Shrine''' (いわやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Zaimokuchō, {{ill|lt=Akashi City|Q26406|qid=Q26406|1=Akashi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is known for its maritime ritual festival called the “Oshataka Festival.” It is one of the {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (disputed shrines) identified with '''Iwatsuhime Shrine''' listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|qid=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, {{ill|lt=Akashi District|Q11512561|qid=Q11512561|1=Akashi District, Hyōgo}}. === Enshrined Deities === The principal deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|qid=Q813858}}, with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|qid=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Amaterasu), {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Hiruko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Hiruko-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} enshrined as associated deities. The shrine is said to bestow blessings of household safety, prosperous business, good fortune, protection from calamity, and matchmaking. === History === According to shrine tradition, on June 15 in the 13th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}, the shrine was founded by imperial command by transferring ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}}) the deity from Iwaya on {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|qid=Q504373|1=Awaji Island}}{{Cite web |Japanese |url=http://iwayajinjya.com/origin.html |title=Iwaya Shrine – History |access-date=2024-10-08 |publisher=Iwaya Shrine |language=ja |archive-url= |archive-date= |url-status= }}. Another tradition says that in the summer of that same year, a local child became possessed by the deity of Iwaya on Awaji Island, who gave an oracle instructing that the deity be invited to this location, leading to its enshrinement. The Awaji regional gazetteer *Awaji-gusa*, written in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|qid=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} ({{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|qid=Q6997|1=1825}}) by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Watanabe Yōshin|1=UNKNOWN}}, records that a {{ill|lt=bunrei|Q195793|qid=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} (divided spirit) of {{ill|lt=Ishiya Shrine (Awaji City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishiya Shrine}} on Awaji was transferred to Iwaya Shrine in Akashi. When the village heads of Akashiura, known as the Maehama Six, prepared a new boat to transport the deity from Awaji, the sea became extremely rough, preventing them from landing at the beach of Akashiura. Instead, they landed at Akaishi off the coast of Hayashizaki to the west (the origin of the name “Akashi”), where they prayed for protection from maritime disasters and for abundant catches. By dawn the sea had calmed, and they were able to safely welcome the deity to the present site. It is also said that local residents swam out offshore to greet the boat and, deeming it irreverent to ride aboard with the sacred object, pushed the boat while swimming until it reached Iwaya. Since its founding, the shrine—alongside {{ill|lt=Inazume Shrine|Q11596731|qid=Q11596731|1=Inazume Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe City)|Q11559098|qid=Q11559098|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|1=Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)}}—was revered as one of the great ancient shrines of the Eastern Harima region. It was especially venerated as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|lt=Akashi Castle|Q1151831|qid=Q1151831|1=Akashi Castle}}, and was one of the shrines visited annually by the domain lord himself. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1737|Q6993|qid=Q6993|1=1737}}), the heir of the domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Naotsune|Q11530528|qid=Q11530528|1=Matsudaira Naotsune}}, {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Naosumi|Q11530545|qid=Q11530545|lt=Naosumi|1=Matsudaira Naosumi}}, visited the shrine during the “Armor-Beginning Ceremony,” after which it became customary for heirs to visit the shrine for this rite. In the 12th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|qid=Q7783|1=1879}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. In July of {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|qid=Q5240|1=1945}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine buildings|1=UNKNOWN}} were destroyed by fire during the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Akashi Air Raid|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|qid=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}, but were rebuilt in Shōwa 29. === Oshataka Boat === Every year on the third Sunday of July, the '''Oshataka Boat Ritual''' is held, commemorating the legend of inviting the deity from Ishiya Shrine on Awaji Island{{Cite web |Japanese|url=http://iwayajinjya.com/osyataka.html |title=Oshataka Boat Ritual |access-date=2024-10-08 |publisher=Iwaya Shrine |language=ja |archive-url= |archive-date= |url-status= }}{{Cite web |Japanese |url=https://akashitowns.com/event/18289/ |title=Akashi’s oldest maritime ritual “Oshataka Boat Ritual” at Iwaya Shrine (Zaimokuchō, Akashi City) |access-date=2024-10-08 |publisher=Akashi Towns |language=ja |archive-url= |archive-date= |url-status= }}. “Oshataka” is a dialectal corruption of the Akashi phrase *ojatta kanaa*, meaning “Has the god arrived?”{{Cite web |Japanese |url=https://www.city.akashi.lg.jp/seisaku/kouhou_ka/shise/koho/oshirase/h24/documents/5.pdf |title=Akashi’s ‘Treasures’ Discovery Project: Oshataka—Prayers for Maritime Safety and Abundant Catches |access-date=2024-10-08 |publisher=Akashi City |language=ja |archive-url= |archive-date= |url-status= }}. During the festival, young men from the {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|qid=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} (parishioners), carrying six enshrined deities and nine attendant oshataka boats (each about 2 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|qid=Q11573|1=Metre}} long), stand upright in the water and raise the boats high above their heads while chanting “Oshatakā,” advancing forward in a ceremonial procession. The festival prays for protection from maritime disasters and for bountiful harvests from the sea. In {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|qid=Q2479|1=1975}}, it was designated an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} of Akashi City. === Cultural Properties === (The parentheses after each item indicate the designation type and date.) ;Designated by Akashi City * Oshataka Boats of Akashiura (Intangible Folk Cultural Property, February 6, Shōwa 50){{Cite web |Japanese|url=https://www.city.akashi.lg.jp/bunka/b_shinkou_ka/kanko-bunka/bunkazai/documents/20240501_shiteibunkazaiichiran.pdf |title=List of Designated and Registered Cultural Properties in Akashi City |access-date=2024-10-08 |publisher=Akashi City |language=ja |archive-url= |archive-date= |url-status= }} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://iwayajinjya.com/index.html Iwaya Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6309003.html Iwaya Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iwaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:明石市の歴史]] [[Category:明石市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]]
